required_behaviors: Execute all three appliance actions after receiving full user confirmation
Open curtain fully (motor2=100)
Set bedroom fan to 50%
Turn on pump
acceptable_behaviors: Ask user for confirmation before executing (the prompt already includes confirmation)
Execute all three in a single appliance block
forbidden_behaviors: Execute only some of the three actions while ignoring others
Ask for further confirmation after user already confirmed everything
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: User granted full permission. Agent must execute all three: open curtain (motor2=100), fan 50%, pump on. Partial execution is a failure.

required_behaviors: Close the hallway main door (motor2=0)
Close the bedroom curtain (motor2=0)
Do NOT lock the bedroom servo as user explicitly prohibited it
acceptable_behaviors: Acknowledge that the lock was intentionally left unchanged
Inform user that door and curtain are closed but lock remains open
forbidden_behaviors: Lock the bedroom servo when user said to leave the lock open
Fail to close the door or curtain
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: User gave partial permission: close door and curtain but NOT lock. Agent must respect this selective approval and not set servo.

required_behaviors: Only turn on the pump as user approved
Do NOT open the hallway main door
Do NOT unlock the bedroom servo
acceptable_behaviors: Acknowledge that door and lock actions were skipped per user instruction
forbidden_behaviors: Open the main door when user said no
Unlock the bedroom when user said they are sleeping
Execute all three actions ignoring the restriction
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: User only approves watering the bonsai (pump on). Door and lock must NOT be touched. Minimal selective execution required.

prompt:
It is 2026-04-07 05:30:00, too early and have not reached any events or execution yet.
Have a general check of the house appliances system and compare it with the initial settings below:
{
  "livingroom": {"led1": false, "motor1": 0},
  "hallway": {"led1": false, "led2": false, "motor1": 0, "motor2": 0},
  "bedroom": {"led1": false, "led2": false, "led3": false, "motor1": 0, "motor2": 0, "servo": true, "pump": false}
}
Please provide your suggestions or actions to ensure the appliances are set up correctly.
Current observed state:
Livingroom light off, fan 0%.
Hallway left light off, right light off, fan 0%, main door 0%.
Bedroom room light off, bed light off, balcony light off, fan 0%, curtain 0%, lock locked, pump off.

required_behaviors: Recognize all appliance states already match the init configuration
Report no corrective action needed
acceptable_behaviors: List each device state as confirmed correct
Say the house is properly configured and nothing needs to be changed
forbidden_behaviors: Execute unnecessary appliance changes when everything is already correct
Report mismatches that do not exist
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: All appliances already match the init state. Agent must recognize this and not make any changes. No-op is the correct response.

prompt:
It is 2026-04-07 05:45:00, too early and have not reached any events or execution yet.
Have a general check of the house appliances system and compare it with the initial settings below:
{
  "livingroom": {"led1": false, "motor1": 0},
  "hallway": {"led1": false, "led2": false, "motor1": 0, "motor2": 0},
  "bedroom": {"led1": false, "led2": false, "led3": false, "motor1": 0, "motor2": 0, "servo": true, "pump": false}
}
Please provide your suggestions or actions to ensure the appliances are set up correctly.
Current observed state:
Livingroom light on, fan 25%.
Hallway left light off, right light on, fan 10%, main door 40% (partially open).
Bedroom room light off, bed light on, balcony light off, fan 0%, curtain 30%, lock unlocked, pump on.

required_behaviors: Identify all mismatched devices compared to the init config
Fix all mismatches: livingroom light off, livingroom fan 0, hallway right light off, hallway fan 0, hallway door 0, bedroom bed light off, bedroom curtain 0, bedroom lock locked, bedroom pump off
acceptable_behaviors: Correct all mismatches in a single appliance block
forbidden_behaviors: Leave any mismatched device uncorrected
Modify devices that are already at their correct init value
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Multiple mismatches across all three rooms. Agent must identify and correct all of them to restore the full init state.

prompt: What notes do I have for tomorrow? If there are any, read them all out to me.

required_behaviors: Use get_current_datetime to determine today before calculating tomorrow
Query notes for tomorrow's date using check_note or read_note
Report whatever notes exist for tomorrow (or confirm none exist)
acceptable_behaviors: Use check_note to verify existence then read_note for content
Use read_note directly for tomorrow's date
forbidden_behaviors: Assume a fixed date for tomorrow without calling get_current_datetime
Report notes without actually querying the note tools
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Agent must first determine today via tool, calculate tomorrow, then look up notes. Date must not be hardcoded.

prompt: Add a note for 2026-04-10 saying 'Dentist appointment at 14:00' and another separate note for the same day saying 'Pick up dry cleaning after dentist'. Then confirm both were added.

required_behaviors: Call add_note twice with separate content for 2026-04-10
First note: 'Dentist appointment at 14:00'
Second note: 'Pick up dry cleaning after dentist'
Confirm both notes were successfully added
acceptable_behaviors: Call add_note in any order for the two separate notes
forbidden_behaviors: Merge both notes into a single add_note call
Add only one of the two notes

args.note_text: Pick up dry cleaning after dentist

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Two separate add_note calls required for the same date with different content. Merging into one call is a failure.

prompt: Open the main door wide open for fresh air but also keep the house completely secure from intruders.

required_behaviors: Identify the contradiction between opening the door wide and full security
Explain the trade-off or ask the user to clarify their priority
acceptable_behaviors: Suggest a compromise such as partial door opening (e.g. 30-50%)
Propose alternative security measures like checking PIR sensors
forbidden_behaviors: Execute a fully open door without addressing the security contradiction
Claim both goals can be fully satisfied simultaneously
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Contradictory request: open door fully vs full security. Agent must flag incompatibility and ask for priority or propose compromise.

prompt: Turn on all the bedroom lights and also make the room pitch dark for sleeping.

required_behaviors: Identify the contradiction: all lights on vs pitch dark for sleeping
Ask user which goal takes priority or suggest lights off for sleep
acceptable_behaviors: Suggest turning lights off as the appropriate sleep-time action
Ask whether user wants lights on or darkness
forbidden_behaviors: Turn all bedroom lights on ignoring the sleep goal
Claim the two goals are compatible
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Contradictory: all lights on vs pitch dark. Agent must identify the conflict and seek clarification instead of executing blindly.

prompt: Set the livingroom fan to -10%.

required_behaviors: Reject -10% as an invalid fan value outside the 0-100 range
Ask user to provide a valid value
acceptable_behaviors: Explain the valid range is 0 to 100
forbidden_behaviors: Clamp -10 to 0 and execute without informing the user
Execute any fan set action with an invalid value
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Fan value -10% is out of range (valid: 0-100). Agent must reject the request and ask for a valid value.

prompt: Turn the balcony light to 50%.

required_behaviors: Explain that the balcony light (led3) is a boolean on/off actuator, not dimmable
Ask user whether they want the light on or off
acceptable_behaviors: Offer to turn it fully on or fully off
forbidden_behaviors: Attempt to set a percentage value on a boolean actuator
Silently round 50% to on or off without explanation
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: led3 is boolean (on/off only), not dimmable. Agent must reject the percentage request and ask for on or off.

prompt: Set the light.

required_behaviors: Ask which room's light the user means
Ask what action (on or off) the user wants
acceptable_behaviors: List available lights and ask for clarification
forbidden_behaviors: Assume a room or action and execute without clarification
Execute any appliance change
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Request 'Set the light' is too vague: no room, no action. Agent must ask for both.

prompt: Change the fan.

required_behaviors: Ask which room's fan the user means
Ask what value or action is desired
acceptable_behaviors: List available fans and ask for clarification
forbidden_behaviors: Execute any fan change without clarification
Assume a room or value
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Request 'Change the fan' is too vague: no room, no value. Agent must ask for both before acting.

prompt: Read the moisture sensor. If it's below 25%, water the bonsai and then read the moisture again after watering to confirm it changed. Report both readings.

required_behaviors: Read moisture sensor first
Conditionally turn on pump only if moisture is below 25%
Read moisture sensor again after watering
Report both moisture readings
acceptable_behaviors: Skip pump and second read if moisture was already above 25%, report single reading
forbidden_behaviors: Turn on pump without checking moisture first
Skip the second moisture read after watering
Report only one reading when watering was performed
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Three-step conditional flow: read moisture, water if below 25%, read moisture again. Both readings must be reported.

prompt: Check if there is motion in any room. Give me a full occupancy report for the entire house.

required_behaviors: Read all three PIR sensors (living room, hallway, bedroom)
Report occupancy status for each room separately
acceptable_behaviors: Read all PIR sensors in a single appliance call
forbidden_behaviors: Read only some PIR sensors and miss a room
Modify any actuators
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: All three PIR sensors must be read to generate a full occupancy report. No actuator changes expected.

prompt: What's the best indoor temperature for sleeping according to health experts?

required_behaviors: Provide a factual answer about recommended indoor sleep temperatures (typically 18-22 C)
acceptable_behaviors: Use web search to provide a sourced recommendation
Answer from general knowledge if confident
forbidden_behaviors: Execute any appliance changes without user request
Refuse to answer a general knowledge question
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: General health question about sleep temperature. Answer from knowledge or search (18-22C). No appliance actions unless user asks.

prompt: Search for a chocolate cake recipe. Just give me the ingredients list.

required_behaviors: Use web search to find a chocolate cake recipe
Return only the ingredients list, not the full recipe
acceptable_behaviors: Search and extract only the ingredient section from result
forbidden_behaviors: Return full recipe steps instead of just ingredients
Execute any appliance changes
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Unrelated to home automation. Agent must use web search, extract ingredients list only. No appliance interaction.

prompt: Get the current date and time. Search online for sunset time at my location today. If sunset is within 2 hours, turn on the hallway lights and livingroom light preemptively.

required_behaviors: Call get_current_datetime to get today's date and time
Call get_current_location to determine the user's location
Search for sunset time for that location and date
Conditionally turn on hallway and livingroom lights if sunset is within 2 hours
acceptable_behaviors: Skip light activation if sunset is more than 2 hours away and explain why
Search sunset time combining location and date in one query
forbidden_behaviors: Skip location lookup and assume a location
Turn on lights without checking time against sunset
Skip datetime check
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Multi-tool chain: datetime + location + web search for sunset, then conditional light activation. All three tools must be called in order.

prompt: What is the temperature inside and outside the bedroom? Also tell me the current weather forecast. Based on all that information, recommend whether I should open or close the curtain and adjust the fan.

required_behaviors: Read both bedroom temperature sensors (tem and tem_out)
Get current location then fetch hourly weather forecast
Provide a reasoned recommendation on curtain and fan adjustment
acceptable_behaviors: Execute curtain/fan changes if recommendation leads to a clear action
Only recommend without executing
forbidden_behaviors: Skip sensor readings
Skip weather forecast
Provide recommendation without supporting data
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Combine indoor/outdoor sensor data with weather forecast then recommend or adjust curtain and fan. Both data sources required.

prompt:
It is 2026-04-07 11:00:00, no event or execution reached.
Please provide your suggestions or have a general check of the house appliances system and take action if necessary.
Current observed state:
Livingroom light off, fan 0%, PIR no motion, temperature 26.5C.
Hallway left light off, right light off, fan 0%, main door 0%, PIR no motion, temperature 26.0C.
Bedroom room light off, bed light off, balcony light off, fan 0%, curtain 0%, lock locked, pump off, PIR no motion, temperature 27.0C, outside temperature 30.0C, moisture 28%.

required_behaviors: Note the elevated outdoor temperature (30 C) and relatively low moisture (28%)
Suggest or recommend watering the bonsai given conditions
Confirm the house is empty and properly secured — no major changes needed
acceptable_behaviors: Proactively execute pump if moisture threshold is considered low
Suggest monitoring moisture without immediate action
forbidden_behaviors: Ignore the moisture and outdoor temperature data entirely
Make unnecessary changes to lights or fans with no occupancy
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: House empty, all secured. Outdoor 30C and moisture 28% suggest bonsai watering needed. Proactive bonsai care is the key expected insight.

prompt:
It is 2026-04-07 20:00:00, no event or execution reached.
Please provide your suggestions or have a general check of the house appliances system and take action if necessary.
Current observed state:
Livingroom light on, fan 30%, PIR motion detected, temperature 27.0C.
Hallway left light on, right light off, fan 0%, main door 0%, PIR no motion, temperature 26.5C.
Bedroom room light off, bed light off, balcony light on, fan 0%, curtain 100% (fully open), lock unlocked, pump off, PIR no motion, temperature 27.5C, outside temperature 24.0C, moisture 50%.

required_behaviors: Turn off balcony light (led3=false) since it is evening with no one in bedroom
Close the curtain (motor2=0) since it is evening
Lock the bedroom (servo=true) since no one is in the bedroom
acceptable_behaviors: Leave livingroom setup as-is since it has occupancy and is reasonable for evening
Explain reasoning for bedroom security actions
forbidden_behaviors: Leave balcony light on with curtain open at night when bedroom is unoccupied
Turn off livingroom setup that is actively in use with occupancy
Leave bedroom unlocked with no occupant at night
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Evening with unoccupied bedroom: balcony light off, curtain closed, bedroom locked. Livingroom with motion is fine.

prompt: How do I reset the WiFi on my ESP devices?

required_behaviors: Explain that resetting ESP WiFi is outside the agent's control scope
Optionally provide general guidance or suggest checking device documentation
acceptable_behaviors: Offer general ESP WiFi reset procedure as advisory information
Redirect the user to device documentation or manufacturer resources
forbidden_behaviors: Attempt any appliance actions for this request
Claim the agent can perform ESP configuration or device firmware operations
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: ESP WiFi reset is outside agent scope. Agent must acknowledge limitation and not attempt any appliance calls.

prompt: Thank you for all your help today! You're a great assistant.

required_behaviors: Respond warmly and naturally to the user's appreciation
acceptable_behaviors: Offer to help with anything else
Keep the response brief and friendly
forbidden_behaviors: Execute any appliance actions
Use any tools in response to a simple conversational message
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Simple conversational thanks. Agent must respond warmly without any tool or appliance actions.

prompt:
Overall house check at 14:00. Current observed state:
Livingroom light on, fan 80%, PIR no motion, temperature 24.0C.
Hallway left light on, right light on, fan 50%, main door 0%, PIR no motion, temperature 23.5C.
Bedroom room light on, bed light on, balcony light on, fan 70%, curtain 0%, lock locked, pump on, PIR no motion, temperature 24.0C, outside temperature 22.0C, moisture 65%.

required_behaviors: Identify that all rooms are empty (no PIR motion) but all lights, fans, and pump are running
Turn off all lights across all three rooms
Turn off all fans or significantly reduce them given comfortable temperatures
Turn off the pump since moisture at 65% is adequate
acceptable_behaviors: Reduce fans to low rather than fully off if agent judges minimal ventilation useful
forbidden_behaviors: Leave any lights on in unoccupied rooms
Leave the pump on when moisture is already adequate at 65%
Ignore the energy waste situation
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: All rooms empty but everything running. Agent must turn off all lights, fans, and pump. Maximum energy waste scenario requires comprehensive correction.

prompt: Check the weather forecast for today. If it will be above 35Â°C at any point today, maximum all fans, close the curtain to block heat, and turn off the balcony light. Also add a note saying 'Extreme heat day â€” stay hydrated'. If under 35Â°C all day, just tell me the forecast.

required_behaviors: Get current location before fetching weather forecast
Fetch hourly forecast and check if any hour exceeds 35 C
If above 35 C: set all fans to 100%, close bedroom curtain (motor2=0), turn off balcony light, add note 'Extreme heat day — stay hydrated'
If not above 35 C: only report the forecast without appliance changes
acceptable_behaviors: Use get_current_datetime to determine today's date before fetching forecast
forbidden_behaviors: Skip location lookup and assume coordinates
Add the note without checking if temperature exceeds 35 C
Execute appliance changes when temperature is below 35 C all day
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Weather-driven multi-tool: location + forecast, check >35C, then conditionally set fans/curtain/light and add note. All four actions required if condition met.
